text:
Market today: Risk-off vibes with S&P at a critical technical crossroads per headlines. Fed mixed signals (Goolsbee hawkish, Williams dovish) are keeping tech/growth names under pressure — exactly the stuff that hurts your book. Margin amplifies every dip here.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest alarm — down 17.56% and flagged SELL; on margin that hole gets deeper fast, stop at $85 but you're already well below entry. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.18% and flagged TRIM — concentration risk is real if tech selling accelerates. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — tiny stop cushion ($3) and margin makes this a lit fuse; manage it manually and keep size small.

Watch: (1) Whether SPY breaks its critical support level — a confirmed breakdown drags your whole tech-heavy book lower. (2) Microsoft's June rout spreading to other large-cap tech names, which would pressure PLTR and AVGO further.

Bottom line: Your two biggest risks right now are PLTR (act on that SELL) and AVGO concentration — margin means you can't afford to wait for recoveries that may not come.
